title: Seminar 1: Inside Innovation Systems for Sustainability Transitions

markdown.headingText: Seminar 1: Inside Innovation Systems for Sustainability Transitions
markdown.markdown:
The goal of this seminar is to provide you with a deeper understanding of why innovation systems are useful analytical tools. ## Learning Objectives ### Functions of Innovation Systems - Understand and describe how the motors of change [@hekkert2007FunctionsInnovationSystems] relate to the seven innovation system functions. ### Systems Thinking: Surprises of Innovation Systems - Realize bounded rationality of roles; actors can act reasonable to them but not to society - Delays in feedback loops make quick reactions difficult ### Sustainability - What is sustainable needs to be consistently negotiated by stakeholders ## Duration 60 minutes of game play, 30 minutes of immediate reflection Rounds: 1. Innovation goals are set 2. Innovators draw cards 3. Outcomes are revealed 4. Discussion of outcomes and state of game 5. Negotiation of innovation goals 6. Innovation goals are set Each innovator draws two cards. They can decide if they from which pile to draw: red stands for product and black for process innovations. When they have a pair, the innovation adds to the scoreboard with the sum of the cards. When they have both a process and a product at the same time, it is doubled. The environmental indicator ranges from 0 - 10; from completely destroyed to a perfectly intact environment. It starts at 10. The economy indicator starts at 100 and goes from there. Each round the environmental indicator decreases by 10% (rounded to the nearest integer) of the economy indicator. Then the lobbyist and the politician decide how to fund the innovators. One card costs 5 output points (average of card deck = 7.5). Each new economic innovation increases the economy indicator by the sum of its numbers. An environmental innovation adds 1 point to the environmental indicator, but adds only half to the economic indicator. Each round the consumer decides if they want to consume a regular product or an environmental product, adding 5 or 3 points to the economy indicator, respectively. ## Overview of Roles | Role Name | Goal | Special Task | Environmental Preference | |-------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------| | Politician | Reelection | Fund University Researcher | | | Industry | Maximize Economic Indicator | Fund Company Researchers and Decide Their Innovation Type | | | Environmental NGO | Maximize Environmental Indicator | | | | Consumer | Maximize Consumption and Environmental Preference | Consume normally for 5 coins;
Consume sustainably for 3 coins. | Care {very much / some / not at all} for environment | ## Instructions
